channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: UK Climate Watchdog Urges Removal of Green Levies From Bills
item.description: The UK government must do more to lower electricity costs, including by removing green levies from energy bills, if it is to meet its net zero emissions target by mid-century, the country’s climate watchdog has said.
item.dc:creator: Jess Shankleman

item.title: Exelon Urges US States to Ease Rules on Utility-Owned Assets
item.description: The head of one of the US’s largest utilities is pressing states across the country to allow it to own generation facilities as it seeks to capitalize on surging demand from data centers, arguing that such a move would help curb rising energy prices for consumers.
item.dc:creator: John Ainger, Emily Forgash

item.title: Trump Administration, Qatar Say EU Methane Rules Threaten Energy Security
item.description: The US, Qatar and other natural gas exporters are urging the European Union to ease some of its pending methane emissions rules, warning that the regulations could threaten the bloc’s energy security.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Liao, Ewa Krukowska, Petra Sorge

item.title: India Urges Crop Shift for Monsoon Planting as Rains Falter
item.description: India is urging farmers in rain-fed areas with limited irrigation to shift to less water-intensive crops such as pulses, oilseeds and millets as authorities prepare for a potentially weak monsoon that could weigh on agricultural output.
item.dc:creator: Pratik Parija

item.title: How Extreme Weather Impacts Consumer Prices Around the World
item.description: Economists are trying to trace the impact of global warming on inflation
item.dc:creator: Emma Court, Kyle Kim

item.title: Canada Backs $504 Million Loan for Indigenous Nuclear Stake
item.description: Canada will make a C$715 million ($504 million) loan guarantee to a group of seven First Nations so they can buy a stake in a large nuclear project east of Toronto.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Kim, Erik Hertzberg

item.title: European Power Prices Jump as Heat Wave Shuts Eiffel Tower Early
item.description: French evening power prices climbed to the highest since the energy crisis of 2022, as a strengthening heat wave breaks temperature records and shuts the top of the Eiffel Tower early.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz, Eamon Farhat

item.title: JPMorgan Says Extreme Heat Is Changing Energy Demand Trends
item.description: As Europeans head into a period of dangerously high temperatures, a team of executives from JPMorgan Chase & Co. says that more frequent heat waves come with profound implications for energy demand.
item.dc:creator: Alastair Marsh

item.title: Trump Seeks a US Nuclear Revival With $17.5 Billion in Loans
item.description: The Trump administration is trying to revive the moribund industry to build large nuclear power plants in the US by offering $17.5 billion in financing for utilities to order equipment for reactors designed by Westinghouse Electric Co.
item.dc:creator: Ari Natter

item.title: Victory of Trump Ally Ends Colombia’s Crusade to Wean World Off Fossil Fuels
item.description: Outgoing President Gustavo Petro banned new drilling and urged the world to decarbonize. His successor, Abelardo de la Espriella, wants “fracking to the max.”
item.dc:creator: Fabiano Maisonnave
